title: Combat oracle guard anim_hash (4968) + flags_onscreen (1568) divergence native-vs-stock on Dam — animation-phase / onscreen visibility

history.note: Both-sides Dam capture (FID-0032 verify), 138 frames. Two dominant guard sub-divergences with distinct root causes from the FID-0054 AI-state cluster: (1) anim_hash diverges on 4968 field-hits — the per-guard animation header state differs, i.e. animation frame/phase cadence is not lock-step native-vs-stock (H3 freeze-timer / H4 ACT_ANIM sub-frame cadence territory). (2) flags_onscreen diverges on 1568 hits — the PROPFLAG_ONSCREEN visibility bit differs because the native renderer and ares Parallel-RDP compute guard on-screen visibility differently (render-dependent; likely parity-divergence, ties to FID-0012 guard-visibility->sim coupling). Both need frame-locked capture + classification. Lower priority than FID-0054 (AI-state) since anim-phase drift is largely cosmetic and onscreen is render-derived.
